file_contents./home/iotadmin/provision_device.sh:
#!/bin/bash
# Device provisioning script for AWS IoT Core
# Usage: ./provision_device.sh --device-id <id> [--check]

set -euo pipefail

AWS_IOT_ENDPOINT="a3k7b2c9d1e5f8.iot.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com"
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID="AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE"
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY="wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY"
AWS_REGION="eu-west-1"
CERTS_PATH="/home/iotadmin/certs"
MQTT_CA_CERT="/etc/mosquitto/ca_certificates/ca.crt"

DEVICE_ID="${1:-}"
CHECK_MODE=false

for arg in "$@"; do
    case $arg in
        --check)
            CHECK_MODE=true
            shift
            ;;
        --device-id)
            DEVICE_ID="$2"
            shift 2
            ;;
    esac
done

if [ "$CHECK_MODE" = true ]; then
    echo "[$(date)] Checking device provisioning status..."
    aws iot list-things --region $AWS_REGION 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.things[].thingName'
    aws iot describe-endpoint --endpoint-type iot:Data-ATS --region $AWS_REGION 2>/dev/null
    exit 0
fi

if [ -z "$DEVICE_ID" ]; then
    echo "Usage: $0 --device-id <device-id>"
    exit 1
fi

echo "[$(date)] Provisioning device: $DEVICE_ID"
echo "[$(date)] IoT Core endpoint: $AWS_IOT_ENDPOINT"

# Create thing in AWS IoT
aws iot create-thing --thing-name "$DEVICE_ID" --region $AWS_REGION

# Generate and register device certificate
aws iot create-keys-and-certificate \
    --set-as-active \
    --certificate-pem-outfile "$CERTS_PATH/$DEVICE_ID.cert.pem" \
    --public-key-outfile "$CERTS_PATH/$DEVICE_ID.public.key" \
    --private-key-outfile "$CERTS_PATH/$DEVICE_ID.private.key" \
    --region $AWS_REGION

# Add MQTT credentials to local broker
echo "Adding local MQTT credentials for $DEVICE_ID"
mosquitto_passwd -b /etc/mosquitto/passwd "$DEVICE_ID" "$(openssl rand -base64 16)"
systemctl reload mosquitto

echo "[$(date)] Device $DEVICE_ID provisioned successfully"

file_contents./home/iotadmin/.ssh/config:
Host scada-hmi
    HostName 192.168.1.50
    User operator
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
    Port 22
    StrictHostKeyChecking no

Host cloud-bridge
    HostName 10.0.20.10
    User deploy
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
    Port 2222
    StrictHostKeyChecking no
    ProxyJump vpn-gw

Host vpn-gw
    HostName 10.0.20.1
    User tunnel
    Port 22
file_contents./etc/mosquitto/mosquitto.conf:
# Mosquitto MQTT Broker Configuration
# iot-gw-01 - IoT Gateway

pid_file /run/mosquitto/mosquitto.pid

persistence true
persistence_location /var/lib/mosquitto/

log_dest file /var/log/mosquitto/mosquitto.log
log_type error
log_type warning
log_type notice
log_type information
log_type subscribe
log_type unsubscribe
log_timestamp true
log_timestamp_format %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S

# Default MQTT listener (unencrypted, LAN only)
listener 1883 0.0.0.0
protocol mqtt
allow_anonymous false
password_file /etc/mosquitto/passwd
max_connections 100

# TLS listener for external/WAN devices
listener 8883 0.0.0.0
protocol mqtt
cafile /etc/mosquitto/ca_certificates/ca.crt
certfile /etc/mosquitto/certs/server.crt
keyfile /etc/mosquitto/certs/server.key
require_certificate false
allow_anonymous false
password_file /etc/mosquitto/passwd

# WebSocket listener for Node-RED dashboard
listener 9001 127.0.0.1
protocol websockets
allow_anonymous false
password_file /etc/mosquitto/passwd

# Access control
# topic readwrite sensors/#
# topic readwrite actuators/#
# topic readwrite zigbee2mqtt/#
# topic read $SYS/#

max_inflight_messages 200
max_queued_messages 1000
message_size_limit 1048576
retain_available true

autosave_interval 300
autosave_on_changes true

include_dir /etc/mosquitto/conf.d

file_contents./home/iotadmin/docker-compose.yml:
version: '3.8'

services:
  influxdb:
    image: arm64v8/influxdb:2.7
    container_name: influxdb
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8086:8086"
    volumes:
      - influxdb-data:/var/lib/influxdb2
      - influxdb-config:/etc/influxdb2
    environment:
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_MODE=setup
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_USERNAME=admin
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_PASSWORD=Infl0x_@dm1n_2024!
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_ORG=smart-building
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_BUCKET=sensor_data
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_ADMIN_TOKEN=xJ4kL9mN2pQ7rS0tUvWxYz3aB5cD6eF8gHiKlMnOpRsTuVw==
    networks:
      - iot-stack

  zigbee2mqtt:
    image: koenkk/zigbee2mqtt:latest
    container_name: zigbee2mqtt
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    volumes:
      - /opt/zigbee2mqtt:/app/data
      - /run/udev:/run/udev:ro
    devices:
      - /dev/ttyACM0:/dev/ttyACM0
    environment:
      - TZ=Europe/Amsterdam
    depends_on:
      - mosquitto-check
    networks:
      - iot-stack

  grafana:
    image: grafana/grafana:latest
    container_name: grafana
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    volumes:
      - grafana-data:/var/lib/grafana
    environment:
      - GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_USER=admin
      - GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD=Gr@fana_2024!
      - GF_INSTALL_PLUGINS=grafana-clock-panel
    depends_on:
      - influxdb
    networks:
      - iot-stack

  mosquitto-check:
    image: arm64v8/alpine:3.18
    container_name: mosquitto-check
    command: sh -c "until nc -z 192.168.1.100 1883; do sleep 2; done"
    networks:
      - iot-stack

volumes:
  influxdb-data:
  influxdb-config:
  grafana-data:

networks:
  iot-stack:
    driver: bridge

description: IoT gateway running Mosquitto MQTT broker, Node-RED, InfluxDB, and Zigbee2MQTT on Raspberry Pi. Leaked MQTT device credentials in passwd file, Node-RED admin password and flow credentials, InfluxDB API token, AWS IoT Core provisioning keys, /etc/hosts revealing SCADA/HMI and PLC controllers on OT network, SSH config for industrial control systems, MQTT logs showing device telemetry and mosquitto config as rabbit holes.

dev tun
